drupalNodeEvent.title: The Apple in my eyes: Chinese leaders’ key phrases defining Sino-Russian Relations (1991-present)

field_speakers.processed: Ziheng Wang (PhD candidate in History at Queens University at Belfast)

field_description.processed: The preseatation is about an analysis of the evolution of Sino-Russian relations through political discourse. Diplomatic language is a powerful tool, and by examining key phrases used by China’s leadership over the past three decades, we can gain deeper insights into China’s evolving strategy toward Russia. The preseatation will explore how Jiang Zemin, Hu Jintao, and Xi Jinping have shaped this relationship through their words, reflecting China’s foreign policy objectives and its response to global geopolitical dynamics.
